title: State maps config to real resources
description: The state file binds HCL addresses to real resource ids and caches attributes; without it Terraform cannot know what it manages.
body: Use when reasoning about imports, moves, or lost state. State records the mapping from each resource address to its real provider id plus a cached copy of attributes; plans diff desired config against this cache (refreshed against the API). Lose or corrupt state and Terraform either re-creates duplicates or orphans real resources. Do not hand-edit state JSON. Actions: manage state with import/mv/rm commands and keep an encrypted, locked, backed-up backend. Validation signals include a plan that matches reality after any state operation. Risk boundary: state contains plaintext resource attributes including secrets, so it is a sensitive artifact — leaking it is a security incident.
